candidates.name: Gavin Newsom
candidates.notes: Two-term governor. Signed nation's strictest gun laws, abortion sanctuary state legislation, banned assault weapons sales. Signed gender-affirming care protections for minors. Opposes school choice. Signed law banning parental notification for transgender students. NRA gave F rating during 2018 campaign. Former SF mayor and Lt. Governor. [2025-26 actions: Signed SB 59 (Trans Privacy Act, court records sealed); SB 497 (trans refuge state strengthened); AB 82/SB 497 (gender-affirming care + abortion confidentiality + shield law). Vetoed 12-month hormone-therapy mandate + AB 1432 (out-of-state abortion/gender-affirming insurance coverage). Mixed signals but aggregate aligns with archetype F across Christian categories.]

profile.religion: Catholic (self-described 'secular Catholic')
profile.background: Entrepreneur and businessman; served as San Francisco mayor (2004-2011) and lieutenant governor (2011-2019) before becoming 40th governor in 2019.

claims.text: Personally championed California Proposition 1 (November 2022) — a joint effort with legislative leaders that amended the California Constitution to enshrine an explicit right to abortion and contraceptives; it passed with over two-thirds of the vote. Also signed a March 2022 law requiring private insurers to cover abortion with no co-pays or deductibles.

As San Francisco mayor in February 2004, directed the city-county clerk to issue marriage licenses to same-sex couples in defiance of California law — roughly 4,000 licenses were issued during what became known as the 'Winter of Love' — a landmark act of institutionalizing same-sex marriage that directly rejected the one-man-one-woman definition the rubric upholds.

Prioritized amnesty provisions over enforcement, blocking any deal that didn't advance a path to legal status for those in the country illegally.

Holds a 100% lifetime score from Reproductive Freedom for All (formerly NARAL Pro-Choice America), received NARAL's endorsement for reelection, led a Senate resolution on medication abortion backed by Planned Parenthood, and visited a Planned Parenthood clinic to push for abortion rights — firmly embedded in the abortion-industry endorsement and funding orbit.

Publicly condemned President Trump's executive orders to end birthright citizenship as unconstitutional, defending automatic citizenship for children born to non-citizens on U.S. soil — opposing the rubric's call to end birthright citizenship as a migration incentive.

claims.text: Carries a consistent A/100% pro-life record scored by SBA Pro-Life America: votes every session to defund Planned Parenthood in federal appropriations, blocks taxpayer-funded abortion domestically and internationally, and opposed any federal legislation to codify Roe v. Wade — affirming protection of the unborn from conception.

Ranked #1 in the entire U.S. House on the National Taxpayers Union scorecard and earned the NTU Taxpayers' Friend Award; signed the Taxpayer Protection Pledge against all tax increases; in 2025 introduced a Line-Item Veto constitutional amendment to curb discretionary appropriations — one of Congress's most consistent fiscal-restraint votes over multiple terms.

Chairs the House Judiciary subcommittee on Immigration Integrity, Security, and Enforcement; in 2026 introduced the Shut Down Sanctuary Policies Act (H.R.7640) to strip federal funding from any jurisdiction that refuses DHS immigration detainers — a direct legislative assault on the sanctuary-city network that shields illegal aliens from removal.

Assembly-era: NRA 'B' average final cycles; Gun Owners of California 'stellar grades' 2016/2018/2020 (cited as 2014 GOC 'win'). Signature legislation: Valley Water Protection Act; HEATS Act (bipartisan geothermal w/ Young Kim R); bipartisan fentanyl crackdown bill (w/ Rep. Evans). Confirmed running 2026; won June 2 primary in CA-13 (will face Kevin Lincoln II R in November; Duarte is not running again).

profile.background: Born September 23, 1977, Merced, California. Central Valley businessman; per TPAction listing, 'businessman and pistachio farmer.' Grew up in family dairy supply / feed store in Central Valley. BA Political Science, UC Santa Barbara; Merced College; Golden Valley HS (inaugural class). California State Assembly District 21 (Merced + parts of Stanislaus) 2012-2022 (term-limited); founded Sacramento bipartisan Problem Solvers Caucus; removed from Government Organization Committee chairmanship in 2020 for opposing Democratic water-policy cuts to San Joaquin Valley irrigation. Previously married to Cadee Condit, daughter of former Rep. Gary Condit; one child. Defeated incumbent John Duarte (R) by 187 votes in November 2024 — last U.S. House race called nationally (Dec 4, 2024). Sworn into 119th Congress January 2025. Blue Dog Coalition Whip; New Democrat Coalition; Future Forum. House Agriculture Committee (Vice Ranking Member, Livestock / Dairy / Poultry Subcommittee); House Natural Resources (Water / Wildlife / Fisheries).

claims.text: Earned the sole endorsement of NARAL Pro-Choice America (now Reproductive Freedom for All) and holds a 100% rating from Planned Parenthood Affiliates of California — placing him inside the abortion-industry advocacy and funding network that the rubric identifies as disqualifying.

Advocates for reinstating the federal assault weapons ban, increasing firearm safety standards, and 'keeping guns out of the hands of violent offenders' through expanded background checks — opposing any constitutional carry or unrestricted Second Amendment protections.

Voted 'No' on H.Res. 1210 (May 2024, condemning the Biden administration's border crisis) and H.Res. 1112 (May 2024, denouncing Biden immigration policies) — opposing calls for border-wall construction, military deployment to the border, and stricter enforcement that the rubric requires.

candidates.name: Sam Liccardo
candidates.office: U.S. Representative CA-16 (sworn in Jan 3, 2025 · 2026 reelection · ex-San Jose Mayor 2015-2022 · former federal prosecutor)
candidates.notes: Heritage Action 119th 0% (lifetime 0%) — orthodox center-left technocrat with strong anti-2A bona fides. Catholic-but-pro-choice on the public record ('Catholic teachings informed his family's personal choices on abortion, every person must follow her own God-given conscience, and the law must allow every person to do just that'). HARD-FALSE life: NAY on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act HR 21 Roll Call 27 Jan 23 2025 (only D YES was Cuellar) + Planned Parenthood Action Fund 2024 primary endorsement + as Mayor proposed and Council approved $250K San Jose grant to Planned Parenthood Mar Monte post-Dobbs (largest such grant in city history). HARD-FALSE marriage: Cosponsor Equality Act HR 15 + Congressional Equality Caucus member + Equality California endorsement. HARD-FALSE family/ed: pro-public-education platform ('bolster public education') + Equality Act SOGI in education. HARD-FALSE border: NAY on ICE/DHS funding extension w/ press release ('ICE has abandoned its mission of removing violent criminals in favor of detaining children, shooting Americans, and terrorizing our communities') + defended San Jose sanctuary publicly to Trump 1.0 ('San Jose Welcomes Migrants'). HARD-FALSE self-defense: as Mayor authored first-in-nation San Jose gun-owner liability-insurance + annual fee ordinance Jan 2022 (worked w/ Brady United / Everytown / Giffords legal teams; city prevailed in federal court Aug 2022) + Everytown for Gun Safety Action Fund 2026 first-round endorsement + ghost-gun / straw-purchase restrictions as mayor + 2021 CNN op-ed supporting federal red-flag + UBC expansion. HARD-FALSE foreign-policy q1: supports continued aid to Ukraine, Israel, and Mexican border per Palo Alto Daily Post — not a restraint-coalition member. Heterodox-D wedges noted: (a) sanctuary violent-felon ICE-notification carve-out as mayor after 2019 Bambi Larson killing (rejected by Santa Clara BOS); (b) Israel arms conditionality + Block the Bombs Act HR 3565 cosponsor; (c) 'Hell No' on OBBBA HR 1 with $3.9T deficit framing; (d) bipartisan OPT-codification w/ Obernolte (R); (e) crypto-trading conflict-of-interest restrictions for Members.

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (cafeteria-Catholic / publicly pro-choice on policy)
profile.background: Samuel Theodore Liccardo, born April 16, 1970, Saratoga CA (Sicilian/Irish/Mexican Californio). Married Jessica Garcia-Kohl 2013. BA Georgetown (Government, magna cum laude, Phi Beta Kappa); JD Harvard; MPP Harvard Kennedy School. Former federal prosecutor (Santa Clara County DA sex-crimes/child-exploitation; DOJ Civil Rights Division). San Jose City Council 2007-2014 (D3); Mayor of San Jose 2015 to Jan 2023. 119th committee: Financial Services (Digital Assets/FinTech/AI, National Security/Illicit Finance, Oversight & Investigations subcommittees). Caucuses: Congressional Equality, Problem Solvers, New Democratic Coalition, Congressional Hispanic Caucus, CAPAC, Ukraine Caucus.
profile.office: 1117 Longworth House Office Building

china.descriptor: China-linked donor tracking. The CCP operates an explicit United Front Work Department to direct overseas members and proxies — including business executives such as Wanxiang Group's Pin Ni (named CCP member 15 times in Chinese state media) — to cultivate political relationships in the United States. RESOLUTE Citizen treats any documented contribution from a CCP-member or United Front-affiliated donor as a failure on america_first/q3.

claims.text: Was the lone dissenting vote against the FY2024 NDAA ($886B) in House Armed Services Committee, stating Pentagon spending was 'on track to a trillion dollar budget' and that defense contractors were 'ripping off American taxpayers at every turn.' In June 2026 introduced H.R.9283 requiring federal review of investment-company acquisitions of controlling stakes in major defense suppliers.

A leading Congressional advocate for ending open-ended military entanglements: voted NO on the House floor against the $886B FY2024 NDAA and has repeatedly called for a 'modern national security strategy' that breaks from forever-war posture — consistent with the rubric's demand to end forever wars.

As of 2021 voted 100% in alignment with President Biden's stated positions, which includes federal protection of abortion access and codifying Roe v. Wade; self-described progressive-capitalist who supports reproductive rights.

candidates.notes: Senior 16-term D-CA-18 Silicon Valley. Heritage Action 119th 9% / lifetime 10%; Inst Legislative Analysis pattern matches; NumbersUSA F- (career, 118th, 119th); NRA-PVF F. HARD-FALSE life: NARAL/Reproductive Freedom for All 100% + cosponsor WHPA HR 12 (119th) + cosponsor EACH Act + PP Action Fund 2022 endorsement; Roe overturn = 'bleak day' statement. HARD-FALSE marriage: YES on Respect for Marriage Act HR 8404 Roll Call 373 (Dec 2022) + HRC 2022 endorsement + Equality PAC board member + Congressional Equality Caucus + Equality Act cosponsor. HARD-FALSE family q2: Equality Act cosponsor codifies SOGI in education. HARD-FALSE election: lead shepherd HR 1 For the People Act + lead shepherd Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act (117th) as House Admin Chair. HARD-FALSE border: NumbersUSA F- + sponsor HR 1511/HR 4696 registry amnesty + NO on Mayorkas impeachment Feb 13 2024 ('complete waste of time') + cosponsor HR 3368 Born in the USA Act blocking Trump birthright-citizenship EO. HARD-FALSE self-defense: NRA-PVF F + co-author HR 4918 Advancing Gun Safety Technology Act (smart-guns) + Gun Records Restoration & Preservation Act cosponsor + opposed HR 38 Concealed Carry Reciprocity in Judiciary markup. **HARD-TRUE foreign_policy_restraint q0: YES on H.Con.Res.38 Iran War Powers Resolution June 3 2026 — 'war requires Congressional consent.'** CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION on Article I. Heterodox-D: vocally opposed AICOA / Open App Markets Act Big-Tech antitrust ('Democratic holdout') — anti-cartel-busting on tech, but rubric q2 specifically targets Big Ag (Bayer/Monsanto/Cargill) so this does not score directly. Israel: YES on Apr 2024 $16B supplemental but signed conditional-aid letter for offensive arms; took small historical AIPAC PAC $ ($2K 2020, $1K 2022); $0 in 2024 cycle.

profile.religion: Lutheran (per Pew Faith on the Hill 117th roster)
profile.background: Susan Ellen 'Zoe' Lofgren, born Dec 21, 1947, San Mateo CA (age 78). BA Political Science Stanford (1970); JD Santa Clara Law (1975). Staffer for Rep. Don Edwards 1970-1978; worked on Nixon impeachment articles drafting as House Judiciary Committee staff 1973-1974 while in law school. San Jose immigration-law partner; San Jose City College board; Santa Clara County Board of Supervisors 1981-1994 (13 years). Only Member of Congress to participate in all three modern presidential impeachments (Nixon staff 1974; Clinton 1998 as member; Trump 2019/2021 as manager). Jan 6 Select Committee full member. 119th: Ranking Member House Science, Space, and Technology; House Judiciary; subcommittees on Courts/IP/Internet, Immigration Integrity, Administrative State/Antitrust.
profile.office: 1401 Longworth House Office Building

candidates.name: Vince Fong
candidates.office: U.S. Representative CA-20 (sworn in May 2024 special · 2024 + 2026 reelection · ex-CA Assembly Vice Chair Approps 2016-2024 · McCarthy district director ~10 yrs)
candidates.notes: Heritage Action 119th 83% / lifetime 87%. Institute for Legislative Analysis 2025 73% / lifetime 68.47% — flagged 'falls short of expectations for a Republican representing CA-20 (R+15)'; Tax & Fiscal 23% + Individual Liberties 33% (~7 points below R caucus 80% average). TPAction overall 75.96 / 2026 session 72.81 — Border 100% + Election Integrity 100% + 2A 100% + Health/Medical Freedom 100% + Educational Freedom 83.33% + American Culture 76.42% + Taxes & Spending **57.14%** (notable weak). Main Street Caucus pragmatist, NOT movement-conservative. Endorsed by SBA Pro-Life 2024, NRA-PVF (lifetime NRA member), CRPA, California Pro-Life Council 2018, AIPAC (>$50K). HARD-TRUE life q0 post-Dobbs statement 'the lives of our most vulnerable is sacred and precious. It is my firm conviction that we must protect the sanctity of life' (KGET) + 'pro-life policies in Congress' (GVWire). HARD-TRUE life q4 SBA Pro-Life endorsement + RFFA 0-of-5 anti-RFFA Assembly record (incl Assembly N on SCA 10 codifying abortion in CA constitution + N on SB 245 free elective abortion coverage). HARD-TRUE biblical_marriage q2 Assembly N on SB 132 trans-inmates-in-women's-prisons. SOFT-FALSE biblical_marriage q4 Assembly Y on AB 2521 Transgender/GNC/Intersex Wellness & Equity Fund Chapter 869 2022 (60-0 Assembly concurrence pass-through — structural, NOT affirmative leadership; per cite-only this is on-record Y for taxpayer-funded TGI programming). SOFT-TRUE family q0 publicly called on Newsom to opt CA into federal Education Freedom Tax Credit + YES on OBBBA HR 1 which contained federal scholarship-tax-credit vehicle. HARD-TRUE family q1 Assembly N on SB 866 minor-vaccines-without-parental-consent. HARD-TRUE family q2 Assembly N on AB 101 Ethnic Studies LGBTQ+ studies vehicle. HARD-TRUE econ q0 YES on HR 1919 Anti-CBDC Surveillance State Act July 17 2025 RC 201 (passed 219-217). HARD-FALSE econ q2 ILA Tax & Fiscal 23% + OBBBA YES adding ~$3-4T per CBO + no BBA cosponsorship. SOFT-TRUE election q1 TPAction Election Integrity 100% (implies SAVE Act / proof-of-citizenship support per TP rubric). SOFT-TRUE border q0 'end illegal immigration' platform + TPAction Border 100% + serves Homeland Security Cybersecurity subcomm. SOFT-TRUE self_def q0 HR 38 Constitutional Concealed Carry Reciprocity 2025 cosponsor. HARD-TRUE self_def q1 TPAction 2A 100% + NRA endorsed + CRPA endorsed + lifetime NRA member. HARD-FALSE foreign_policy q3 AIPAC officially endorsed + >$50K AIPAC-affiliated PAC giving per TrackAIPAC + 'We stand with Israel' Instagram. SOFT-FALSE industry_capture q0 authored AB 1064 signed Newsom Oct 8 2021 expanding pharmacist authority to independently initiate + administer FDA-approved vaccines for age 3+ without prescriber protocol (pro-access posture, not informed-consent). HETERODOX-CONSERVATIVE-CONCERNS: Main Street Caucus chairmanship trajectory + ILA + TPAction fiscal weakness flag this as a centrist-R seat-holder, not a movement-conservative; pro-life is firm but pro-vaccine-access posture cuts the other way on industry-capture.

profile.religion: Protestant (Wikipedia member infobox; no specific congregation or personal Christ-profession publicly stated)
profile.background: Vincent Cheng Fong, BA Political Science UCLA; MPA Princeton (Woodrow Wilson). Married Amanda Boschma March 11 2023 (met at Bakersfield Angels foster-care nonprofit); at least one son. Worked as district director for Kevin McCarthy ~10 years before Assembly. CA State Assembly Dec 2016-May 2024 (AD 34 then AD 32, Vice Chair Appropriations). Won McCarthy seat May 2024 special; reelected Nov 2024 (65.1% def. Boudreaux). 119th committees: Science Space & Technology (Environment + Investigations subcomm); Transportation & Infrastructure (Aviation + Railroads/Pipelines); Homeland Security (Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Protection). Caucuses: Western Caucus + Dutch Caucus (co-chair) + **Main Street Caucus** (centrist R); NOT Freedom Caucus or Republican Study Committee.
profile.office: 243 Cannon House Office Building

aipac.descriptor: AIPAC operates a super-PAC (United Democracy Project) that spends millions per cycle on US races on behalf of a foreign government's policy agenda. RESOLUTE Citizen treats documented contributions as a failure on america_first/q3.

foreign_policy_restraint.q4.reason: Auto-flipped by foreign-influence adjustment: profile.score_adjustments.aipac is set, which is direct evidence of the failure described by america_first q4.

profile.confidence_note: US House member bulk-scored via party-default archetype (maga_conservative_r). Per RESOLUTE Citizen methodology, silence on a major v4.0 issue is itself signal — a House member who has not publicly opposed a major issue is scored False on the affirmative-action question. Individual evidence review is pending; when specific votes/statements get promoted via apply-claims.py, this confidence will upgrade to archetype_curated. Run: rescore-house-by-party.py (2026-05-18). 2026-05-18 evidence: applied Iran war powers vote (H.Con.Res.38, 3/5/2026) — voted NAY per roll call source. 2026-05-18 evidence: voted YEA on Laken Riley Act (S.5/H.R.29, signed 1/29/2025). 2026-05-18 evidence: voted YEA on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (H.R.21/S.6, Jan 2025). 2026-05-18 evidence: voted YEA on SAVE Act citizenship verification for voter registration (H.R.22, 4/10/2025). 2026-05-18 evidence: voted YEA on NDAA FY26 (HR5371/S1071, 12/10/2025) — $900B defense + $400M Ukraine + Europe troop limits. 2026-05-18 evidence: voted YEA on Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (H.R.28/S.9, Jan/Mar 2025). 2026-05-18 evidence: voted YEA on Protect Children's Innocence Act (H.R.3492, 12/17/2025) — federal criminal ban on gender-affirming care for minors. · 2026-05-20 — Default-flagged as incumbent_seeking_reelection (no explicit status).

claims.text: A lifetime member of the National Rifle Association; in the California Assembly authored a bill providing firearm safety training materials in multiple languages — extending lawful-carry literacy to California's non-English-speaking communities — and maintains a pro-Second-Amendment record opposing new restrictions on firearms.

Voted for the Laken Riley Act (H.R.29, signed into law January 29, 2025) — the first legislation enacted in the 119th Congress — requiring DHS to detain any illegal alien arrested for theft, burglary, or violent crimes, ending catch-and-release for criminal aliens and aligning with a mandatory-enforcement posture.

candidates.name: Jim Costa
candidates.office: U.S. Representative CA-21 (sworn in Jan 3, 2005 · 11th term · 2026 reelection · former Blue Dog Coalition Communications/Admin/Policy Chair · Ranking Member House Ag Livestock/Dairy/Poultry)
candidates.notes: Heritage Action 119th 0% / lifetime 9% (118th 5%, 117th 0%, 112th 22%). ILA 2025 30.77 / lifetime ~18%. NumbersUSA F career / 117th F-. NRLC 20% historical. Equality California 100% (2018, 2019; endorsed 2020). PPAF endorsed 2026. Multi-decade D-line voting with Blue Dog crossovers DOCUMENTED but most don't map cleanly to TRUE direction cells: Laken Riley HR 29 YES Jan 7 2025 = detention not deportation; Saudi/Yemen 2018 YES to BLOCK war-powers vote = anti-Article-I-restraint (FALSE); Iran June 2026 = co-introduced WEAK ALTERNATIVE resolution with Gottheimer permitting ~one-month Iran hostilities (Common Dreams + Hill coverage) = NOT the restraint resolution (FALSE on q0); PLCAA 2005 YES to gun-maker civil immunity (historical, pre-rubric era); Secure Fence Act 2006 + Minuteman 2006 = historical, contradicted by current Dream Act sponsorship; Al Green censure Mar 6 2025 YES (1 of 10 Ds) + DC criminal code rejection Feb 9 2023 + Keystone XL 2015. HARD-FALSE life: opposed Dobbs ('strips women of their freedom'); WHPA HR 3755 117th original cosponsor; NO on HR 3 No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion Act 2011; YES expanding embryonic stem-cell research Jan 2007 + May 2005; PPAF endorsed 2026 + NARAL endorsement history. HARD-FALSE marriage: 2006 NO on constitutional 1m1w marriage amendment + Equality CA 100% 2018/2019/endorsed 2020 + Equality Act HR 5 cosponsor (117th) + RFMA HR 8404 YES + Equality Caucus member + 2007 YES SOGI job nondiscrimination + 2009 YES anti-gay hate crimes. SOFT-FALSE family q2: NO on HR 5 Parents Bill of Rights Roll Call 161 Mar 24 2023 (D-bloc Nay) + Equality Act cosponsor codifies SOGI in K-12. SOFT-FALSE christian_liberty q2: Equality Act cosponsor extends SOGI compelled-speech regimes into employment/education. SOFT-FALSE election q1+q2+q3: cosponsored John R. Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act / Freedom to Vote Act + voted to pass bundling (expands automatic + same-day registration + mail-ballot access). HARD-FALSE border q1: original cosponsor American Dream and Promise Act HR 6 (Dreamer pathway) + Farmworker Modernization Act. HARD-FALSE self-defense q0: supports universal background checks + sponsored bill mandating background checks on private transfers Mar 2021. HARD-FALSE self-defense q1: original cosponsor AWB Act 2021 + voted for HR 2377 Federal Extreme Risk Protection Order Act (federal red-flag) + YES on Bipartisan Safer Communities Act 2022. SOFT-FALSE foreign_policy q0: June 2026 co-introduced WEAK ALTERNATIVE Iran war-powers resolution with Gottheimer/Cuellar/Landsman/Panetta permitting ~one-month hostilities — Blue Dog crossover deferring to executive war-making, contrary to Article-I restraint. HARD-FALSE q1: 1 of only 5 Ds joining all Rs to BLOCK Yemen War Powers vote 2018 (Newsweek + Wikipedia). profile.background: James Manuel Costa, born April 13 1952 Fresno CA. Portuguese-Azorean third-generation dairy farmer; San Joaquin Memorial HS 1970; BA Political Science CSU Fresno 1974. CA Assembly 1978-1994 (elected at 26 — sponsored Costa-Hawkins Rental Housing Act); CA Senate 1994-2002; President NCSL 2000-01. U.S. House: old CA-20 (2005-2013), CA-16 (2013-2023), CA-21 (2023-present). Eloped with Kelsey end of 2020. Family dairy operation defaulted on ~$9M loan + seized by bank June 2018. 119th committees: Agriculture (Ranking Member Livestock/Dairy/Poultry + Forestry/Horticulture); Foreign Affairs (Europe subcommittee). Caucuses: Blue Dog Coalition + New Democrat Coalition + Problem Solvers + Hispanic Caucus + Portuguese Caucus (co-chair) + Equality Caucus + Crime Survivors/Justice Caucus (co-founder) + Ukraine Caucus + Taiwan Caucus.

claims.text: A self-described liberal Roman Catholic who nonetheless is an original co-sponsor of the Women's Health Protection Act, which would create a federal right to abortion overriding most state limits; opposed the Dobbs ruling, saying it 'strips women of their freedom to make their own decisions' — rejecting any life-at-conception standard.

A member and former chair (116th Congress) of the Blue Dog Coalition, whose foundational mission is fiscal conservatism: the coalition explicitly advocates for balanced-budget discipline and restraint on deficit spending — aligning with the rubric's anti-deficit/balanced-budget standard.

candidates.name: David Valadao
candidates.office: U.S. Representative CA-22 (sworn in Jan 3, 2013 · 6th term · 2026 reelection · Chair Republican Governance Group/RG2 Dec 2024)
candidates.notes: Heritage Action 119th 46% / lifetime 47% — **bottom-quartile R caucus** (117th 74% for context). The New American Freedom Index lifetime 48% (119th 60% / 118th 35% / 117th 63%). SBA Pro-Life A+ + RFFA 0%. NumbersUSA D+. NRA-PVF historically endorsed 2016. **Chairman Republican Governance Group (RG2)** — explicit moderate-GOP caucus chairmanship elected Dec 2024. Multiple CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS DOCUMENTED: (a) RFMA HR 8404 YES Roll Call 373 July 19 2022 (1 of 47 R crossovers) federalizing SSM; (b) Trump 2nd impeachment YES Jan 13 2021 (1 of only 10 House R impeach voters) — cited Trump rhetoric as 'un-American, abhorrent, and absolutely an impeachable offense'; (c) DIGNIDAD Act 2025 HR 4393 cosponsor pathway to legal status for ~10-12M illegal immigrants + voted AGAINST 2014 GOP DACA dismantling + YES Dream and Promise + YES Farm Workforce Modernization + June 2018 family-separation opposition; (d) Pro-IVF on the record (debate quote 'I do support IVF'); (e) Removed cosponsorship from Life at Conception Act in 2024 during competitive race (Ms. Magazine + 19th News); (f) Refused federal 15-week abortion ban ('left up to the state'); (g) OBBBA YES July 3 2025 RC 173 218-214 despite signing April 2025 letter against deep Medicaid cuts (key swing vote in highest-Medicaid-enrollment R district at 64%); (h) Mayorkas impeachment YES Feb 2024 (with R majority). HARD-TRUE life q0 debate quote 'I am pro-life. I do support the exceptions. Rape, incest, the life of the mother. And I do support IVF' + SBA A+. SOFT-FALSE life q1 refused federal 15-week ban + dropped Life at Conception cosponsorship 2024. **HARD-FALSE life q2** pro-IVF on the record (embryo creation/discard implicated). SOFT-TRUE life q4 EMILY's List specifically targets him as opposition + SBA A+ + no PP/NARAL/EMILY's List PAC giving in cited sources. **HARD-FALSE biblical_marriage q0 + q1 CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION**: RFMA HR 8404 YES Roll Call 373 July 19 2022 codifying SSM federally. HARD-TRUE biblical_marriage q2: YES on HR 28 Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act Roll Call 12 Jan 14 2025 amending Title IX to bar biological males from women's sports. SOFT-FALSE biblical_marriage q4: 2016 YES on banning LGBT-discrimination by federal contractors + RFMA YES + declined to sign 2015 Obergefell amicus (mixed; net leans FALSE on LGBTQ-promotion opposition). SOFT-FALSE econ q2: ILA Tax & Fiscal pattern + OBBBA YES adding to deficit + no BBA cosponsorship. SOFT-TRUE border q3: HR 2 includes mandatory E-Verify (with ag carveout caveat from Farm Workforce Modernization cosponsorship). SOFT-FALSE foreign_policy q1: Ukraine aid YES + 'stands with Ukraine' (cont supplemental Apr 2024). profile.background: David Goncalves Valadao, born April 14 1977 Hanford CA. Portuguese-American Azorean immigrant family; fluent Portuguese + Spanish. Married high-school sweetheart Terra 1999; three children; Hanford CA. Hanford HS 1995; College of the Sequoias part-time 1996-1998 (no degree). Co-partner Valadao Dairy (1992); family operation faced ~$9M loan default 2018 + bank seized June 2018 + $325K wage-violation settlement unpaid via bankruptcy. CA Assembly District 30 (2010-2012; def. Fran Florez 61-39). U.S. House: elected 2012 (CA-21), lost to TJ Cox 2018, won back 2020, reelected 2022 (CA-22 redistricted) + 2024. 119th committees: Appropriations (3 subcomm) + previously Budget. Caucus memberships: **Chairman Republican Governance Group (RG2/Tuesday Group) Dec 2024** explicit moderate-GOP caucus + Republican Main Street Partnership + Problem Solvers + Climate Solutions + Congressional Armenian Issues (co-chair) + American Sikh (co-chair) + Western Caucus.
profile.office: 2465 Rayburn House Office Building

claims.text: Holds an A+ rating from SBA Pro-Life America and stated 'I am proud to receive an A+ rating from @SBAList for my pro-life voting record.' Voted YES on HR 1 (One Big Beautiful Bill Act) which defunded Planned Parenthood of Medicaid dollars for one year.

Voted YES on HR 2, the Secure the Border Act of 2023, and HR 5525, the Continuing Appropriations and Border Security Enhancement Act of 2024 — a consistent record of backing border-wall and enforcement legislation.

Supports a legislative path for Dreamers/DACA recipients to remain in the United States: 'These young people make significant contributions to our communities each and every day… Congress must provide a legislative solution so these individuals may continue to live in America — the only home they know.' This rejects the mandatory-deportation standard.

profile.background: Jay Phillip Obernolte, born Aug 18 1970 Chicago IL; raised Fresno CA; resident Big Bear Lake CA since 1997. BS Engineering & Applied Science Caltech (1992); MS Artificial Intelligence UCLA (1997 — note: this is an MS not a PhD per Wikipedia + House bio); DPA Public Administration California Baptist University (2020). Founded FarSight Studios 1990 (family-friendly video games ~25 employees, still owner/president/technical director). Married Heather Obernolte 1996, two sons (Hale + Troy). Big Bear Lake City Council 2010 then Mayor. CA Assembly AD-33 Dec 1 2014 – Nov 30 2020. U.S. House since Jan 3 2021 (CA-08 in 117th, CA-23 from 118th). 119th committees + caucuses: Republican Study Committee + Republican Governance Group (Tuesday Group successor — explicit moderate-R caucus) + Main Street Caucus + Congressional Western Caucus + co-chair House Artificial Intelligence Task Force (with Ted Lieu D, 2024) + Chair House Republican Policy Committee (assumed April 15 2026 — formal House R leadership role). NOT in Freedom Caucus.

claims.text: Holds an SBA Pro-Life America A/100% rating: votes to stop federal taxpayer funding of abortion, opposed codification of Roe v. Wade into federal law, and supports the Dobbs ruling returning abortion regulation to states — a consistent legislative record against federally-mandated abortion access.

Introduced a Balanced Budget Amendment to the U.S. Constitution as his very first bill in Congress; voted for the Fiscal Responsibility Act of 2023 — billed as the largest reduction in nondefense discretionary spending in U.S. history — publicly attributing chronic inflation to reckless federal deficit spending.

On July 19, 2022, voted for the Respect for Marriage Act as one of 47 House Republicans, encoding federal recognition of same-sex marriages into statutory law; his vote ensures same-sex marriage remains legally protected federally regardless of any future Supreme Court ruling — contrary to the one-man-one-woman standard the rubric upholds.

china.descriptor: China-linked donor tracking. The CCP operates an explicit United Front Work Department to direct overseas members and proxies — including business executives such as Wanxiang Group's Pin Ni (named CCP member 15 times in Chinese state media) — to cultivate political relationships in the United States. Federal law prohibits CCP members from donating to U.S. campaigns, and most CCP-member donors hold green cards rather than citizenship, raising significant legal questions. RESOLUTE Citizen treats any documented contribution from a CCP-member or United Front-affiliated donor as a failure on america_first/q3.

The CCP operates an explicit United Front Work Department to direct overseas members and proxies — including business executives such as Wanxiang Group's Pin Ni (named CCP member 15 times in Chinese state media) — to cultivate political relationships in the United States. Federal law prohibits CCP members from donating to U.S. campaigns, and most CCP-member donors hold green cards rather than citizenship, raising significant legal questions. claims.text: Voted against the House National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA), explicitly citing its restrictions on abortion access for servicemembers as grounds for opposition — rejecting any reduction in abortion availability and meeting no standard of legal protection for life from conception.

Led the Therapeutic Fraud Prevention Act (H.R. 4340, 118th Congress, 2023), which would federally prohibit licensed therapists from helping LGBTQ+ individuals align their sexual orientation or gender identity with biological reality — characterizing any such effort as 'harmful sham' therapy and directly opposing the rubric's position of upholding biological sex distinctions.

Applauded passage of the Bipartisan Safer Communities Act (2022) as a 'commonsense gun safety package,' endorsing its expanded background checks, enhanced juvenile records access, and red-flag law incentives — supporting new federal firearm restrictions contrary to the rubric's defense of Second Amendment rights against new bans and restrictions.

claims.text: Cosponsor of the DIGNIDAD Act (HR 4393, 119th Congress), which would create a pathway to legal permanent resident status for up to 12 million illegal immigrants — directly opposing the mandatory-deportation standard. The bill, led by Rep. Maria Elvira Salazar, drew 20 Republican cosponsors including Kim.

Voted YES on HR 1 (One Big Beautiful Bill Act) on May 22, 2025, which included a one-year defunding of Planned Parenthood of Medicaid dollars — a pro-life budget provision tracked by SBA Pro-Life America.

candidates.name: Ken Calvert
candidates.office: US Representative · CA-41 → CA-40 post-Prop-50 redistricting (Riverside County / Coachella Valley · sitting since 1993 · House Appropriations Defense Subcmte CHAIR · advanced June-2-2026 top-two primary against Young Kim R, Joe Kerr D, Esther Kim Varet D)
candidates.notes: Federal evidence finalization 2026-06-05. HEADLINE CROSS-AISLE CATCH — biblical_marriage Q1+Q0 FALSE: Calvert was among the 47 House Republicans who voted YES on H.R.8404 Respect for Marriage Act (roll 373 7/19/22 + roll 513 12/8/22) — codifying same-sex marriage federally. Palm Springs redistricting reportedly the political driver. He had voted FOR DOMA in 1996. This is a substantive deviation from the conservative biblical-marriage axis on a top-tier marquee vote and the highest-value finding of this batch. CONFIRMED TRUE on rest of Pillar A + election integrity + border: SOL pattern (Vote Smart pro-life key-votes; SBA scorecard page exists but numeric blank — null Q3 endorsement), BM Q2 (Equality Act NO 2021), FCS Q0+Q1 (H.R.5 Parents Bill of Rights COSPONSOR + YES roll 161 3/24/23), CL Q1 (Equality Act NO doubles as religious-liberty), ES Q0 (Limit Save Grow Act YES + Heritage 74%/61%/87%), EI Q1 (SAVE Act YES roll 345 9/11/24) + Q0 OBJECTED to AZ/PA electors Jan 6 2021 (opposite directional vote from Kim — Calvert objected to certification, statement defending objection citing 'forensic investigation'), BI Q0 (HR2 YES — his Legal Workforce Act / E-Verify language rolled into HR2) + Q1 (Laken Riley YES roll 6) + Q3 (E-Verify by HR2 inclusion), 2A Q0+Q4 (NRA-PVF 2020 'A' endorsement letter; Bipartisan Safer Communities Act NO roll 299 6/24/22 — not among 14 GOP defectors). CROSS-AXIS NEGATIVE on Pillar B: (1) FP entire axis FALSE — as Defense Approps Chair he INTRODUCED + managed the Israel Security Supplemental Feb 2024 + voted YES on April 2024 $95B supplemental roll 151 + 'Supports Security Funding for Israel/Ukraine/Indo-Pacific' press release; he is the GOP defense-supplemental pusher, opposite of restraint; (2) IC Q4 FALSE — defense-industry capture documented at extraordinary scale per OpenSecrets: ~$390K from defense sector 2022 cycle (11% of $3.3M raised), ranked #2 House recipient of defense PAC/individual money, top contributors RTX/BAE/Leidos/Lockheed (LMT alone $70.3K in 2020), #2 Misc Defense + #3 Defense Electronics — combined with direct gavel control over the defense topline = textbook industry-capture overlap. NULL where evidence thin: FCS beyond HR5, CL beyond Equality Act NO, ES beyond FRA single vote, EI Q2+Q4, BI Q2+Q4, 2A Q1-Q3. SBA Pro-Life numeric / current-cycle NRA-PVF letter / FRC-AFA numerics did not surface. Advanced June-2-2026 primary against Kim — both R incumbents in CA-40.

claims.text: Rated 100% by the National Right to Life Committee; supported the overturning of Roe v. Wade, stating it 'shifts the power to set abortion policies to Congress and to the States' — consistent with the rubric's life-at-conception standard.

Voted YES on HR 2, the Secure the Border Act of 2023; stated 'The worst step we can take is to grant amnesty to people who entered our country illegally — it sends a horrible message to those who entered our country legally' — aligning with wall-and-enforcement priorities.

Has stated 'I continue to have serious concerns about our federal debt. As this graph shows, the leading driver of debt is our mandatory spending' — a sustained anti-deficit position consistent with the rubric's balanced-budget standard.

candidates.notes: Federal evidence finalization 2026-06-05. Textbook progressive D Heritage Action 0%/8% lifetime. HEADLINE FALSE wall: SOL Q0+Q4 FALSE (Planned Parenthood Action Fund 2024+2026 endorsement); BM Q0+Q2+Q4 FALSE (Equality California + LGBTQ Victory Fund endorsed; first openly gay immigrant in Congress; Equality Caucus member; reintroduced International Human Rights Defense Act); FCS Q1+Q2 FALSE (Dem-bloc NO on H.R.5 Parents Bill of Rights — 213-208 party-line); CL Q1 FALSE (Equality Caucus + anti-religious-exemption framing); EI Q1 FALSE HARD (on-camera MSNBC opposition to SAVE Act 'all about Donald Trump') + Q0 (led Oversight Dem DC Jail Jan-6-defendant 'insurrectionists' visit); BI Q0+Q2 FALSE HARD (HR2 Secure the Border NAY 5/11/23 roll 209 — only 2 Ds crossed; as Mayor directed Long Beach PD not to cooperate with ICE civil enforcement; Jan 24 2026 publicly called to 'abolish ICE' after Alex Pretti killing + opened ICE-violence-examination hearing as Oversight Ranking Member); 2A Q0+Q1+Q4 FALSE HARD (Giffords endorsed; LEAD SPONSOR Stop Militarizing Our Streets Act with Raskin + Andy Kim endorsed by Brady/Everytown/Giffords/Guns-Down/MFOL; introduced AMMO Act requiring federal licenses + background checks for ammunition sellers/buyers). FP genuinely SPLIT (no conservative-side TRUE): YES on $95B Israel/Ukraine/Indo-Pacific supplemental (interventionist FALSE on restraint) BUT also supported H.R.3565 LIMITING offensive arms transfers to Israel (left-of-AIPAC posture) — net no conservative cross-aisle catch, score FP NULL where ambiguous. INDUSTRY CAPTURE: Oversight Ranking Member antagonist-posture but no specific Big-Pharma/Big-Ag/Pentagon-audit cosponsor isolated — high-bar NULL across IC. NO CROSS-AISLE CATCHES — textbook progressive D from coastal CA district who votes party line. Advanced June-2-2026 primary.

claims.text: The first openly gay immigrant elected to Congress and first openly LGBT mayor of Long Beach; co-chairs the House International LGBTQI+ Rights Task Force (119th Congress), which explicitly promotes LGBTQ identity as a protected class in U.S. foreign and domestic policy — rejecting the one-man/one-woman standard.

Voted in favor of all three April 2024 foreign military aid supplementals totaling ~$95B for Ukraine, Israel, and Taiwan — supporting continued open-ended U.S. military entanglements abroad rather than restraint.

Supports abortion rights and has backed legislation to protect federal abortion access, consistent with his Congressional Progressive Caucus membership and 100% alignment with Planned Parenthood's legislative agenda.

candidates.name: Maxine Waters
candidates.office: US Representative · CA-43 (South LA / Inglewood · sitting since 1991 · Ranking Member House Financial Services 117th-119th · led June-2-2026 primary at ~63% over Cristian Morales R 19% / Myla Rahman D 13% / David Sedlik D 4%)
candidates.notes: Federal evidence finalization 2026-06-05. Senior House progressive D Heritage 0% — archetype FALSE wall confirmed with HARD cites across Pillar A + EI + Border + 2A. HEADLINE FALSE HARD: SOL Q0+Q1+Q2+Q4 FALSE (NARAL / Reproductive Freedom for All 100% lifetime; EMILY's List endorsed; voted to eliminate Hyde restrictions); BM Q0+Q1+Q2+Q4 FALSE (YES on H.R.8404 RFMA roll 513 12/8/22; PFLAG 2025 National Champion of Justice; Equality Act long-time supporter; HRC endorsed); FCS Q1+Q2 FALSE (Dem-bloc NO on H.R.5 roll 161 3/24/23 — party-line context HARD); CL Q1 FALSE (Equality Act supporter); ES Q0+Q4 FALSE (NO on McCarthy debt-ceiling 2023; called IRA 'shameful' for too-small housing spend, wanted MORE not less); EI Q1 FALSE (SAVE Act Dem-bloc NO + public call for VRA floor vote); BI Q0+Q2 FALSE (H.R.2 NAY roll 209 — zero D crossover; June 2025 confronted armed Nat-Guard at LA ICE raids/protests in sanctuary posture); 2A Q0+Q1+Q4 FALSE HARD (100% gun-violence-prevention rating; 2019 public call to 'ban assault weapons or kick our a—out of Congress'). TWO HARD CROSS-AISLE CATCHES (narrow but documented): (1) FP Q1 TRUE — voted NAY on H.R.8034 Israel Security Supplemental 4/20/24 roll 152 — among ~40 D NAYs vs 366-58 passage + publicly called for 'immediate cease-fire in Gaza' (anti-blank-check restraint posture on Israel axis); caveat: she did sign separate pro-Israel resolution + supported Ukraine aid → score Q1 TRUE but Q0 NULL; (2) IC Q4 mixed — Wall Street oversight chair record (Wells Fargo 'recidivist' hearings, Equifax breach hearings, megabank-breakup bill, financial industry 'braced for her ascent') is a documented anti-capture record on financial-services axis — but the IC rubric q-cells are pharma/Big-Ag/Pentagon-focused, not Wall Street. To avoid mis-mapping, do not score IC cells TRUE on Wall Street evidence; high-bar NULL the IC q-cells but note the financial-capture record in notes. Additional nuance: EI Q0 — she OBJECTED to Trump electors Jan 6 2017 (parallel to GOP 2021 objection pattern). This is process-democratic-norm-breaking on both sides — null Q0 rather than scoring (her 2017 objection is documented but RESOLUTE rubric direction on Q0 hand-counted-paper-ballot doesn't map). Advanced June-2-2026 primary ~63%.

candidates.notes: Federal evidence finalization 2026-06-05. CRITICAL CALIBRATION FINDING: The pre-research hypothesis that Correa would be a textbook Blue-Dog cross-aisle correction did NOT hold. His Blue-Dog co-chair role, Catholic faith, and Border Security Ranking Member seat suggested deviations — but on the marquee R bills he voted with the D caucus every time. Quote: 'I'm a Catholic. I have my personal views on that, but I am also a member of Congress who represents my constituents' (Wikipedia). NRA-PVF F, NARAL 100%, SBA Pro-Life F, Heritage Action 0%/5%. The genuine 'deviation' is mildly hawkish tone on border-technology legislation he himself introduced (passed committee with GOP support) — but his ROLL-CALL pattern is standard D. Cosponsor of both RFMA H.R.8404 (HARD YEA both passages) AND Equality Act H.R.5 117th (HARD YEA per own press release). H.R.5 Parents Bill of Rights NAY + amendment to bar immigration-status disclosure at schools. H.R.2 NAY (own press release as Ranking Member opposing). Laken Riley NAY (own FB statement: 'bad public policy'). BSCA YES with full D caucus. SAVE Act NAY (own press release + 2026 successor opposition). Trump 2nd impeachment YEA. Jan 6 he hid in the gallery with a gas mask + supported impeachment + 25th Amendment invocation. Correa is the Blue-Dog-brand-without-the-Blue-Dog-vote-record case. NO marquee cross-aisle catches found.

candidates.name: Steve Padilla
candidates.notes: Appointed to Senate in 2021 (replaced Kamala Harris), elected 2022. First Latino senator from California. Former CA Secretary of State. Heritage Action 0% current session. Strong gun control advocate. Co-sponsored pathway to citizenship legislation. Opposes border wall. Supports Equality Act.

profile.religion: Catholic
profile.education: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (BS Mechanical Engineering)
profile.background: Former California Secretary of State and Los Angeles City Council member. Engineer by training. First Latino to represent California in U.S. Senate. Appointed in 2021 to fill Kamala Harris's seat.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-20 — competitive district challenger (CA-22). Archetype: establishment_d.
profile.religion: Sikh (Punjabi Sikh immigrant family Delano CA; authored 2023 bill recognizing 1984 Sikh genocide)
profile.background: Jasmeet Kaur Bains, born July 2, 1985 in Delano, CA to Punjabi Sikh immigrants from India. B.S. Biology Illinois Institute of Technology (2006); M.D. American University of Antigua (2013); residency Clinica Sierra Vista Bakersfield 2015-2018. Family physician Adventist Health Delano; previously chief medical officer California Medical Assistance Team Central Valley. Elected CA Assembly AD-35 (Bakersfield/Delano/Wasco/Arvin/Shafter/McFarland — Kern County) Nov 2022 with 60% jungle defeating Dem Leticia Perez; re-elected Nov 2024 with 57% over R Robert Rosas. First South Asian American woman + first Sikh American elected to CA Assembly. Termed out of Assembly 2026. Launched CA-22 congressional campaign July 16, 2025 citing Valadao's OBBBA YES + Medi-Cal cuts. Heavy fentanyl record (4 authored bills + $11M secured for Kern County).

candidates.name: Diana Becton
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HETERODOX-HIGH-VALUE crossover — the genuine Adam-finds-the-crossover moment. NET: 1 HARD TRUE + 1 SOFT TRUE vs 8 HARD FALSE + 1 SOFT FALSE on 11 cite-able cells of 30 (matches existing 11-of-30 answered display); 19 NULL out-of-tier or insufficient evidence per cite-only discipline. HEADLINE: Becton is a publicly-identified, sermon-preaching, theology-teaching ORDAINED BAPTIST MINISTER (Reverend) holding M.T.S. Pacific School of Religion 2015 + preaches Sundays at Bethlehem Missionary Baptist Church Richmond (husband Rev. Dr. Alvin Bernstine's pulpit) + teaches Christian + Black Theology at Allen Temple Baptist Oakland LIAT since 2015 (Prof of Theological Studies). HARD TRUE christian_liberty q0 — not a private faith: she campaigns from the pulpit and rallies from it. SOFT TRUE christian_liberty q4 — basic non-hostility test met; does NOT hide ministry, on-dais Christian identity. PAINFUL TENSION (preserve both as cited HARD facts): SAME minister rallied courthouse May 14 2022 with quotes 'Access to safe abortions is a public safety issue. I will always, proudly, stand by our right to privacy and our right to choose!' and 'A woman's right to choose is fundamental. It is non negotiable.' — HARD FALSE life q0. June 25 2022 (post-Dobbs): 'I will not pursue charges against anyone who seeks abortion in Contra Costa County — no matter what state they reside in.' Signed FJP 83-prosecutor joint statement — HARD FALSE life q1. Touted Planned Parenthood endorsement May 2022 — affirmative single-issue endorsement bloc. 5-OF-5 HARD FALSE public_justice cluster: q0 signed 2018 Brennan/FJP 'newly-elected progressive prosecutors' cohort statements; founding/early FJP-affiliated DA; Soros-network beneficiary $600K+ across cycles via Justice & Public Safety PAC; Andrew Hall conviction (former Danville deputy, Laudemer Arboleda shooting) — first Contra Costa officer convicted in on-duty shooting, took 2+ years to charge — the sword IS swung but SELECTIVELY against police. q1 stopped prosecuting low-level drug crimes; established drug diversion; juvenile diversion expansion through age 25; restrictive civil-asset-forfeiture policy; 'race-blind charging' / Vera Institute partnership 2019 — Capital B News and Davis Vanguard characterize as 'California's most progressive prosecutor' post-Pamela-Price-recall. q2 public on-record support for bail reform / cashless-bail per Death Penalty Information Center podcast. q3 April 2026 Davis Vanguard op-ed 'Punishment Alone Fails to Serve Victims, Restore Communities' — explicit reimagining-accountability framing. q4 public, on-record opposition to death penalty (DPIC podcast guest specifically on ending death penalty); resentencing initiative reduces sentences on closed cases; recall organizers cite victims'-families abandonment. SOFT FALSE biblical_marriage q4 — endorsed by Color of Change PAC / Voting While Black, ACLU-aligned PAC support. Recall died Oct 2025 (missed Sept 25 2025 signature deadline, needed 72,556 valid sigs). KEY CORRECTIONS TO PRIOR BRIEF: (1) JD = Golden Gate University School of Law NOT Pepperdine; (2) denomination = historically-Black Baptist NBC-USA orbit NOT Disciples of Christ NOT COGIC; (3) church name = Bethlehem Missionary Baptist Church Richmond (husband's pulpit) + teaches at Allen Temple Baptist Oakland LIAT — no McGlothen Temple connection; (4) 2026: NOT on June 2026 ballot, next election 2028; (5) MTS Pacific School of Religion 2015 is real and confirms christian_liberty q0 HARD TRUE; (6) spouse Rev. Dr. Alvin Bernstine pastor Bethlehem MBC Richmond since 2006, married 2020. Spouse-controversy disclosure (not scored, contextual): Rev. Dr. Bernstine was named in 2022 civil suit alleging underage sexual abuse at his prior church; Becton publicly defended him — not her conduct, not scoreable, logged for public-record file. Pete Wilson 1995 judicial appointment is notable cross-party-history flag.

profile.religion: Ordained Baptist minister, Reverend. Historically-Black Baptist tradition (NBC-USA orbit). Preaches Sundays at Bethlehem Missionary Baptist Church Richmond CA (her husband Rev. Dr. Alvin C. Bernstine's pulpit since 2006). Teaches Christian Theology and Black Theology at the Leadership Institute at Allen Temple (LIAT) — Allen Temple Baptist Church Oakland — since 2015; holds Professor of Theological Studies title. M.T.S. Pacific School of Religion (Berkeley, progressive ecumenical seminary) 2015. NOT Disciples of Christ; NOT COGIC; NOT Pepperdine — prior brief was incorrect on denomination, JD, and church.
profile.education: B.A. Economics, San Francisco State University; J.D. Golden Gate University School of Law; M.T.S. (Master of Theological Studies), Pacific School of Religion 2015.
profile.background: District Attorney, Contra Costa County CA (D, second-term incumbent) — appointed Sept 2017 by BoS to fill Mark Peterson vacancy; won 2018 first-election majority no-runoff; RE-ELECTED June 7 2022 56% vs Mary Knox (37-yr veteran Deputy DA); current term through Jan 2027 (next election 2028). Born Aug 16 1951 (age 74). Resident El Sobrante CA. Prior: 22 years Contra Costa Superior Court judge (appointed 1995 by Gov. Pete Wilson — a REPUBLICAN appointment); elected Presiding Judge 2011; past president National Association of Women Judges. Two adult sons from prior marriage. Married Rev. Dr. Alvin C. Bernstine 2020 (senior pastor Bethlehem MBC Richmond). RECALL EFFORT: filed paperwork March 2025, missed Sept 25 2025 signature deadline (needed 72,556 valid sigs), officially ended October 2025.

claims.text: Endorsed by NARAL Pro-Choice California (now Reproductive Freedom for All) as a 2022 statewide candidate, and Planned Parenthood publicly congratulated her upon appointment as California Secretary of State — placing her inside the abortion-industry endorsement-and-funding network.

As a California Assembly member, Weber was described by Equality California as 'consistently one of the most outspoken champions for LGBTQ+ civil rights,' securing laws requiring LGBTQ+-inclusive curriculum in public schools; Equality California endorsed her 2022 SOS re-election campaign — promoting LGBTQ ideology in schools and policy in ways the rubric opposes.

candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: 43rd LA Mayor since December 12 2022; CURRENT term through 2026 (reelection cycle 2026 unverified-in-pass). PARTY confirmed D. RELIGION: Baptist biographical only — no mayoral-dais Christian-public-profession event → christian_liberty NULL per cite-only. FEDERAL-record FLOOR per LOCAL discipline: most US House CA-33/CA-37 floor votes 2011-2022 do NOT score for LOCAL mayoral cells unless ratified by mayoral action — federal NARAL 100% + Planned Parenthood 100% + Everytown + HRC 100% lifetime NOT scored at LOCAL. 5 HARD FALSE + 1 HARD TRUE LOCAL profile (heterodox-D HIGH-VALUE on public_justice axis): sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE from mayoral LA reproductive-rights march WITH VP Kamala Harris explicitly headlined as pro-abortion-rights mayoral advocacy (NBC LA) + NARAL-endorsed mayoral candidate + Keep LA Pro-Choice mayoral petition — LOCAL mayoral public-advocacy event ratifies federal pro-choice record on q1 abortion-abolition-advocacy axis. biblical_marriage q2 FALSE from sustained mayoral support for 'protecting access to gender-affirming care' for trans youth + April 2024 mayoral visit to TransLatin@ Coalition (TransLatin@ Coalition direct documentation) + LA Transgender Advisory Council maintained under her tenure — direction: affirmative trans-ideology support at mayoral level. biblical_marriage q4 FALSE from June 4 2024 SIGNED Council motion to fly Pride Flag over LA City Hall for FIRST TIME IN HISTORY (mayor.lacity.gov direct documentation) with quote 'now more than ever, we must stand together' — historic-first affirmative LGBTQ+ promotion in public policy. economic_stewardship q2 FALSE from $1B FY25-26 deficit + initially proposed 1,600+ layoffs then walked back + $1B/4-yr LAPD raise contract Dec 2023 + Inside Safe Executive Directive 2 sustained spending + ED 1 affordable-housing accelerator (LAist + The Center Square + LA Public Press) — pattern of deficit growth not structural reform; opposes deficit-spending-restraint framework. border_immigration q1 FALSE from cash-aid-to-illegal-immigrants proposal July 2025 (signalscv) — direction: institutionalized municipal migrant-aid infrastructure opposing mandatory-deportation framework. border_immigration q2 FALSE from Dec 9 2024 SIGNED Ordinance 188441 codifying LA as sanctuary city explicitly 'prohibits use of any City resources, personnel, property and data to support federal immigration enforcement' + July 11 2025 Executive Directive 17/12 banning ICE from staging on city property + Immigrant Affairs Liaisons + Abbott migrant-bus 'abhorrent' framing — affirmative sanctuary posture against ICE cooperation. public_justice q0 HARD TRUE — HIGH BAR CLEARED: SIGNED $1B/4-yr LAPD raise package Dec 2023 + FY24-25 +$138M LAPD + FY25-26 $3.3B LAPD allocation + restored hiring levels with funding for 240 additional recruits + on-dais sought-the-raises rationale 'to better recruit and retain officers' (ABC7) — affirmative back-the-blue advocacy documented by LA Public Press headline 'Karen Bass went from fighting cops to funding them while LA's budget burns' verifying the reversal from prior defund-adjacent rhetoric. NOT scored sanctity_of_life q0/q2/q3/q4 (no conception/embryonic-stem-cell/euthanasia/PP-PAC statement at mayoral level beyond fed-record-FLAG), biblical_marriage q0/q1/q3 (no marriage-definition/civil-unions/no-fault-divorce statement at mayoral level), family_child_sovereignty q0-q4 (LAUSD relationship with Sup Carvalho is fire-recovery-coordination only — no mayoral school-choice/parental-notification/CRT/age-verification/faith-adoption statement located), christian_liberty q0-q4 (Baptist biographical only — no mayoral-dais Christian-public-profession or conscience-exemption defense statement located), economic_stewardship q0/q1/q3/q4 (no CBDC/sound-money/usury/ESG-opposition statement), election_integrity q0-q4 (City Clerk runs LA municipal elections — mayoral cells N/A per LOCAL discipline; no Bass election-administration statement located), public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4 (no anti-cashless-bail/anti-Soros-DA/anti-public-health-excuse statement — Bass did NOT endorse Hochman against Gascón; that was Caruso), refuse_state_overreach q0-q4 (Bass opposes Trump from LEFT — National Guard deployment June 2025 + Marines deployment + opposes Newsom from LEFT — encampment-clearing order; NEITHER maps to Christian-conservative-coded state-overreach refusal vector → NULL per cite-only rubric; June 2024 floated 'mask ban' for protesters in Jewish-leader context = mayoral hint not state-overreach refusal), border_immigration q0/q3/q4 (no physical-barrier/E-Verify/birthright-citizenship statement at mayoral level), self_defense q0-q4 (no LA-mayoral 2A-affirmative action located; Federal Everytown/Moms-Demand endorsement is FEDERAL FLAG not LOCAL), foreign_policy_restraint + industry_capture + refuse_federal_overreach (mayoral-rare). Sole-non-budget crisis-leadership negative signal (Jan 4-8 2025 Ghana trip during Palisades Fire Jan 7 ignition + 'It was a mistake to travel' admission per ABC7) does NOT map cleanly to any rubric cell → narrative-only flag for Adam review. Net 5 HARD FALSE + 1 HARD TRUE on cited cells; HETEROODOX-D HIGH-VALUE on public_justice axis — Bass is the cleanest LOCAL-tier mayoral 'defund-to-fund' reversal located in this cycle's CA cluster. Recommend revisiting after FY26-27 LAPD budget cycle + 2nd-Trump-term ICE-enforcement posture stabilizes + 2026 reelection-cycle posture verification.

SFUSD-jurisdiction caveat — family_child_sovereignty q0/q1 NULL because SFUSD governed by independent elected board. SPARSE-DATA: 17 months in office; recommend rescore pass at 2-year mark Jan 2027. HETERODOX FLAGS catalog: (1) $1 salary; (2) $643M deficit-closure budget; (3) Fentanyl State of Emergency Ordinance 10-1 BoS; (4) ended fentanyl-supply distribution without treatment requirement; (5) Rebuilding the Ranks 425-officer directive; (6) 97-arrest drug-market blitz + expanded Tenderloin raids; (7) public DA partnership with Brooke Jenkins; (8) encampment 324→162 in year one; (9) endorsed by SF Briones Society (R-leaning); (10) condemned 'Tax the Jews' chant Feb 2026; (11) vocally opposed federal-troops intervention; (12) PRC flag-raising 9/30/2025 + China-US Friendship Day. profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-20 — Mayor of San Francisco. Archetype: establishment_d. · 2026-05-21 — v5.0 new-category seed (progressive lean inferred from existing God First pattern; party-default archetype, not individual evidence).
profile.religion: Jewish (Reform). Member of Congregation Emanu-El, a Reform synagogue in San Francisco. Son of Rabbi Brian Lurie (former executive director of the Jewish Community Federation of San Francisco) and Mimi (Ruchwarger) Lurie. NOT CHRISTIAN — christian_liberty cells partial direct-action scoring only per Jewish-mayor protocol.
profile.education: Town School for Boys + University High School (SF) → B.A. Political Science Duke University 1999 → M.P.P. Goldman School of Public Policy UC Berkeley 2005.
profile.background: 46th Mayor of the City and County of San Francisco CA (D, first term) — sworn Jan 8 2025 after defeating incumbent London Breed in Nov 5 2024 RCV race ~56%-43% in final round; current term to Jan 2029. Heir to Levi Strauss fortune via stepfather Peter E. Haas (Haas family); first cousin once removed of U.S. Rep. Dan Goldman (D-NY). Personal net worth est. $8-47M (SF Chronicle Feb 2025). Career: Bill Bradley 2000 IA field organizer → Robin Hood Foundation NYC 2001 → Founded Tipping Point Community 2005 (raised >$500M, stepped down as CEO Nov 2019 remained board chair) → chaired SF 2016 Super Bowl Host Committee. Married Becca Prowda 2006 (she is CA Director of Protocol under Gov. Newsom); two children. Born 1977. Self-funded ~$8M+ of ~$16M total campaign; FIRST SF mayor elected with no prior government experience since 1911. CAPPED OWN PAY at $1/year (declined full $364,582 mayoral salary).

candidates.name: Todd Gloria
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Classic-orthodox-D LOCAL profile. NET: 1 HARD TRUE + 2 SOFT TRUE vs 14 HARD FALSE + 3 SOFT FALSE on 20 cite-able cells of 30; 10 NULL per cite-only discipline. 14 HARD FALSE multi-vector cross-anchor: sanctity_of_life q0 (Dobbs response 'very dark day') + q1 (Oct 2022 press conference IN FRONT of Planned Parenthood with PP President Darrah Johnson endorsing SD City Council resolution on Prop 1; June 2024 buffer-zone ordinance banning pro-life sidewalk-counselors within 8 ft); biblical_marriage q0 (openly gay; lives with male partner Adam Smith) + q2 (Aug 2023 REMOVED Pastor Dennis Hodges from Citizens Advisory Board on Police/Community Relations after Hodges abstained from 'condemn transphobia' letter on Christian conviction — Gloria memo: Hodges 'has made repeated concerning public comments about LGBTQ people — specifically, the transgender community'; Hodges' federal §1983 suit pending) + q4 (June 7 2021 RAISED Progress Pride Flag over San Diego City Hall for FIRST time in city history with quote 'The rainbow flag flying proudly at City Hall for the first time sends an important message to our LGBTQ community: we see you, we support you and you belong here'; created standing Mayoral LGBTQ Advisory Group; launched city-funded Pride Promenade construction); christian_liberty q0 + q2 (same Hodges removal cross-anchor — the predicate act was refusing to sign 'condemn transphobia' letter on biblical conscience; classic religious-conscience-vs-LGBT-affirmation collision) + q4 (Pride flag raising + Pride Promenade public-works investment + Prop 1 city resolution); refuse_state_overreach q0+q1+q2+q3 (Dec 1 2021 imposed vaccine mandate on ALL city employees with statement 'the proposed vaccine mandate for City employees is an issue of public safety for both the community and employees'; 629 officers initially unvaccinated, 130 LEFT THE FORCE; Aug 2 2021 indoor mask rule for all City of Phoenix facilities; July 2025 EO explicitly 'reaffirms the City's alignment with SB 54' Calif. Values Act — affirmatively aligned WITH state framework); border_immigration q1+q2 (July 2025 Executive Order: SDPD 'does not and shall not engage in immigration enforcement'; prohibits sharing license-plate-reader data; Oct 2025 City Council passed Due Process & Safety Ordinance Act codifying EO; 'deeply upset' by ICE operation at SD Italian restaurant); self_defense q0+q1 (Sep 2021 signed E.N.U.F. Ordinance banning possession/purchase/sale/receipt/transportation of non-serialized firearms and unfinished frames/receivers; misdemeanor / $1,000 / 6 months in jail; defended against Firearms Policy Coalition + SD County Gun Owners PAC suit). 3 SOFT FALSE: family_child_sovereignty q2 (SD Public Library hosts annual 'Pride Storytime' branded under Drag Queen Story Hour SD, city-funded LGBTQIA+ Library Services program, no parental-opt-out posture from mayor); economic_stewardship q2 (FY26 budget faced $258M structural deficit; Voice of San Diego retrospective headlined 'Disappointment Follows Gloria Into Sixth Year'; mixed record — uses cuts/efficiencies but persistent gap large); public_justice q4 NULL (DEI-office-creation does NOT cleanly map to Rom 13:4 sword cell; held NULL not FALSE per cite-only). HETERODOX CATCHES: HARD TRUE public_justice q2 — June 29 2023 signed Unsafe Camping Ordinance with on-dais quote 'there has to be consequences for illegal behavior in the city'; 3-encounter progressive enforcement with misdemeanor escalation — unusually firm for coastal-D mayor, drew progressive activist protest at his office. SOFT TRUE public_justice q3 — FY26 SDPD +4% YoY ($702.3M); May 2025 revised budget restored Northwestern Division patrols. SOFT TRUE refuse_state_overreach q4 — Feb 2023 proposed ending COVID-19 emergency declaration + sunsetting city employee vax mandate in step with state. DEI-OFFICE-PARADOX flag: created standalone Chief Race & Equity Officer position Aug 2021 BUT proposed eliminating Race & Equity Office in 2025 amid deficit (folding into HR for ~$460K savings) + VETOED council attempt to restore it June 2025 — fiscal pragmatism > DEI orthodoxy in this one cell. Net SOFT FALSE because he created it in the first place. Religion NULL: no church/faith affiliation found; USD attendance institutional only.

claims.text: Wiener authored California SB 107 (2022), making California the first 'transgender-minor sanctuary state' — barring enforcement of out-of-state laws restricting gender-affirming care for minors. Signed into law September 2022 and took effect January 2023.

As Chair of the California Legislative LGBTQ Caucus and a San Francisco Democrat, Wiener has consistently supported abortion-rights legislation and been endorsed by reproductive-rights organizations; he has voted YES on every major California abortion-access expansion bill since joining the State Senate in 2016.

Wiener has consistently backed California's assault-weapons bans, magazine-capacity limits, and background-check expansions as a California state senator from San Francisco; he holds an A rating from the Giffords Law Center and has opposed every effort to loosen California's strict gun laws.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-20 — competitive district challenger (CA-22). Archetype: establishment_d.
profile.background: Rodolfo 'Rudy' Salas Jr., born March 12, 1977 in Bakersfield, CA; Mexican-American Central Valley farm-labor family ('worked his way from laboring in the fields with his father'). UCLA BA history/political science (2000). One year as VP Al Gore WH staffer; CSU Bakersfield Upward Bound counselor; district director for State Sen. Dean Florez 2004-2010. Bakersfield City Council Nov 2010 (first Latino in 112-year council history). CA Assembly AD-32 Dec 3 2012 – Dec 5 2022 (5 terms; Moderate Caucus leader; Business & Professions Committee chair until stripped April 2017 in retaliation for SB 1 gas-tax NO vote per Bakersfield Now + CA Political Review). Lost CA-22 to David Valadao 51.5%-48.5% (2022) and 53.4%-46.6% (2024). Filed FEC paperwork for 2026 3rd attempt + listed by Ballotpedia News Mar 9 2026 + endorsements page rudysalas.com/endorsements live — but DID NOT APPEAR on the June 2 2026 jungle primary ballot (CA SoS results show only Valadao, Bains, Villegas, Garcia, and one minor candidate). No public withdrawal-statement located in this research pass; CSO believed to have backed Bains instead.

candidates.name: Randy Villegas
candidates.office: U.S. Representative CA-22 (2026 D Primary Candidate · Visalia Unified School Board trustee Area 6 since Dec 2021 · College of the Sequoias political-science professor · UCSC PhD politics · Bernie/AOC/DSA-aligned progressive)
candidates.notes: Race standing 2026-06-05: LEADING for #2 slot (29.8% / 12,234 vs Bains 25.7% / 10,529 — ~1,300-vote margin, uncalled by AP, ~46% ballots outstanding). KEY ENDORSEMENTS: Bernie Sanders (Nov 2025) + Ro Khanna + Pramila Jayapal + Dolores Huerta + CA DSA + Working Families Party + UAW + CTA + Indivisible + Congressional Progressive Caucus PAC + Our Revolution + Leaders We Deserve (David Hogg) + BOLD PAC. NOT endorsed by Planned Parenthood Action Fund (PPAF endorsed Bains instead) — left abortion position unstated on Issues page. FEC H6CA22190: zero AIPAC/DMFI PAC money + 94.4% individual donors + only 5.6% PAC share of $1.75M raised. HARD-TRUE foreign_policy_restraint cross-cluster (q0+q1+q3): Issues page Foreign Policy 'No More Endless Wars — Whether it's Iraq, Afghanistan, or Palestine — Americans are sick of endless wars' + 'when it comes to war or bailing out other countries, we must get our priorities straight' + ActBlue 'Randy Rejects AIPAC' explicit fundraising vehicle (https://secure.actblue.com/donate/randy-rejects-aipac) + Jan 2024 Gaza ceasefire letter signatory + Common Dreams quote affirming Gaza-as-genocide. HARD-TRUE industry_capture q2 (Issues page Environment 'corporate polluters … gas leaks, air pollution, water contamination… I will fight to hold corporate polluters accountable' + Issues page Jobs 'Break up monopolies … No corporations should be allowed to become large enough to control an entire sector' — Big-Ag/anti-trust framing, Central-Valley-specific). HARD-FALSE marriage q4 (Mar 10 2026 VUSD board pro-LGBTQ statement re Redwood HS homophobic-slur photo: 'We must fight for the dignity, and the safety, of all of our LGBTQ students, all of our staff, and our community here in Visalia and beyond'). HARD-FALSE family q0 (CTA endorsed + universal-childcare/free-public-college/'raise wages for teachers/custodians/nutritional/bus drivers as VUSD trustee' platform — opposite of universal school choice). HARD-FALSE family q2 (2022 quote: 'I'm really excited about the opportunity to expand courses like ethnic studies and make it a graduation requirement' — pro-ethnic-studies-as-graduation-requirement = pro-CRT-curriculum-mandate). HARD-FALSE econ q4 (Issues page: 'Tax the top 1% of income earners and wealth holders' + 'End corporate ownership of single-family homes' + Medicare for All + price caps on drugs — explicit redistributionist progressive economics opposite of opposing WEF/ESG/anti-monopoly direction, though the SAME anti-monopoly direction TRUE-aligns on industry_capture q2). NO Issues-page section on life/marriage/2A/election-integrity/IVF/AB-1955/AB-775/CBDC/Fed-audit/balanced-budget/voter-ID/SAVE-Act/border-barrier/E-Verify/birthright/constitutional-carry/red-flag/AWB/NFA/Iran-WPR/AUMF/WHO/NCVIA/PREP/raw-milk/Pentagon-audit — NULL per evidence-first rubric (Issues-page silence is honest evidence-absence; he is letting endorsers signal positions rather than stating them himself). profile.background: John Scott Duarte, born September 9, 1966, Modesto CA; Portuguese-American. Wife Alexandra (m. 1996); 4 children. Lives on pistachio/almond farm in rural Stanislaus County. BA Finance, San Diego State (1989); MBA, University of the Pacific (Eberhardt). Won open CA-13 seat 2022 by 564 votes (last U.S. House race called nationally that cycle); LOST 2024 reelection to Adam Gray (D) by 187 votes (last race called Dec 4 2024). DECLINED to run for 2026 reelection; endorsed Javier Lopez (R, Mayor of Ceres) in 2026 R primary, then switched endorsement to Kevin Lincoln II when Lopez withdrew. Self-described in Sept 2023 Fresnoland interview: 'I'll stand against the woke. I'll stand against the ultra-conservatives.'

profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-20 — Mayor of Los Angeles. Archetype: establishment_d. · 2026-05-21 — v5.0 new-category seed (progressive lean inferred from existing God First pattern; party-default archetype, not individual evidence).
profile.religion: Devout Roman Catholic — Knight Commander of the Order of St. Gregory the Great bestowed by Pope Francis Jan 14 2017 at Cathedral of Our Lady of the Angels by Abp José Gómez; led consecration/expansion of Our Savior Catholic Church at USC; major donor to Catholic causes; Loyola HS Board Chair.
profile.education: Pepperdine University School of Law J.D. 1983 (undergraduate degree at USC).
profile.background: Founder/Executive Chairman, Caruso (real-estate developer — The Grove + Americana at Brand + Palisades Village + Commons at Calabasas + Rosewood Miramar Beach hotel). Former real-estate attorney at Finley Kumble. PRIOR CIVIC POSTS: LA Department of Water & Power Commissioner (youngest ever, age 26); LA Police Commission member 2001-2005 + LA Police Commission PRESIDENT 2001-2003 (appointed by Mayor James Hahn); USC Board of Trustees member from 2007 + USC Board CHAIR 2018-2022; Loyola HS Board Chair; Ronald Reagan Presidential Foundation & Institute Board of Trustees. PARTY HISTORY (4 flips in 11 yrs): R (long-time) → Decline-to-State 2011 → R again March 2016 ($240K to Kasich super-PAC vs Trump) → Independent 2019 → D Jan 2022 (~3 weeks before LA Mayor filing). 2022 LA MAYOR RACE: lost general 55-45 to Karen Bass after spending >$100M of personal money. 2026 CANDIDACY: Jan 16 2026 publicly announced he will NOT seek any 2026 public office (neither LA Mayor nor CA Governor) per Westside Current + LAMag — focus on Steadfast LA wildfire-recovery nonprofit. FAMILY: wife Tina Caruso (philanthropist + Gigi C swimwear co-founder), married since 1987 (~37 yrs); four children — Alex, Gregory, Justin, Gianna.
